description_long: This bungalow features a low, rectangular footprint with a hip roof covered in dark brown shingles. The exterior walls are finished in a light beige or tan stucco with darker trim along the base and corners, giving it a clean, modern appearance. Windows are symmetrically placed on the longer facades, with three on one side and two on the opposite, all featuring dark frames and visible curtains. The front entrance is marked by a small, covered porch with a gabled roof extension and two supporting columns. A single chimney rises from the roof near the rear, and a small side extension is visible. The overall design is compact and practical, suitable for suburban or urban neighborhoods.
description: A single-story, hip-roofed bungalow with beige stucco walls, dark trim, and a small covered front porch. Symmetrical windows and a central chimney complete its modern, suburban look.
description_view_0: This view shows the side of the bungalow with three large windows and the hip roof sloping evenly, highlighting the dark trim and beige walls.
description_view_1: The rear view displays a plain wall with no windows, emphasizing the uninterrupted roofline and the building's rectangular shape.
description_view_2: This side features two windows and a small roof extension, with the chimney visible near the back corner.
description_view_3: The front view reveals the main entrance with a small covered porch, a single window to the right, and the gabled porch roof standing out against the hip roof.
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical side windows with single front window
